content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES

TODD AND SIEGEL
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

DOUGLAS LORD TODD
SAMUEL SIEGEL
SCOTT E. NICHOLS

4909 LAKEWOOD BOULEVARD, SUITE 300
LAKEWOOD, CALIFORNIA 90712-2405
TELEPHONES
(213) 531-1805
(213) 531-1806

TELECOPIER
(213) 634-0405

October 9, 1990

PLEASE REPLY TO:
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
4909 Lakewood Boulevard, Suite 300
Lakewood, California 90712
(231) 634-1217

California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Blanca Breeze

Re: Potential Conflicts of Interest for City of Pico Rivera
Planning Commissioners

Dear Ms. Breeze:

The City Attorney of the City of Pico Rivera is requesting written
advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) concerning
potential conflicts of interest for three Planning Commissioners in
the City of Pico Rivera.

The City of Pico Rivera Redevelopment Agency is currently in the
process of expanding the Project Area of the existing Redevelopment
Agency, and adopting a Redevelopment Plan for the expanded area.
During the course of this procedure, three City of Pico Rivera
Planning Commissioners have advised our office of factual
situations which may give rise to potential conflicts of interest
pursuant to Government Code Section 87100-87103. Therefore, the
City Attorney is requesting a written opinion from the Fair
Political Practices Commission as to whether each of the Planning
Commissioners would have a conflict of interest and be disqualified
from participating in Pico Rivera Redevelopment Agency Projects
based on the following factual situations.

Commissioner Angel S. Alvarado's wife works parttime for the Cigna
Health Plan which is located within the expanded Project Area of
the Pico Rivera Redevelopment Agency. Mrs. Alvarado earns in
excess of $10,000 per year from Cigna Health Plan. Cigna Health
Plan is listed on the New York Stock Exchange, and owns the
building in which it is located.

Commissioner Jess Zapien owns a business within 300 feet of the

Fair Political Practices Commission
October 9, 1990
Page 2

expanded project area.  The business is incorporated and 100% of
the stock is owned by Mr. Zapien and his wife.  The nature of the
business is a vehicle registration service which processes vehicle
registrations through the Department of Motor Vehicles for its
customers.  95% of its customers are auto dealerships which are
outside of the city of Pico Rivera.  5% of his business is with
local residents within the City of Pico Rivera and also partly
within the expanded Redevelopment Area.  There are no automobile
dealers within the City of Pico Rivera although the City has tried
to obtain automobile dealerships for the past ten years.  It is
unlikely that there will be any automobile dealerships within the
City of Pico Rivera in the foreseeable future.  The property on
which Mr. Zapien's business is located is leased by Mr. Zapien for
a period of five years, and there is one year remaining on the
lease.

Mr. Zapien also owns a residential lot which is 17,600 square feet
in size.  He is currently building a new residence on this
property which he intends to occupy with his family.  The property
is located more than 300 feet, but within 2500 feet of the expanded
Project Area.  Mr. Zapien states that he cannot reasonably foresee
that the project would increase or decrease his property value by
$10,000 or more.  The majority of residential lots in the City of
Pico Rivera are 6000 square feet.  Approximately 10% of the lots in
the City are in excess of 15,000 square feet.

Commissioner Steve Quiroz owns 1,000 shares of Pacific Western
National Bank.  Pacific Western National Bank is located within the
expanded project area.  Pacific Western National Bank Stock is not
listed on the American Stock Exchange.  There are approximately
400,000 shares of stock outstanding.  For 1989, the assets of the
Bank were $75,100,000, the pretax income of the Bank was $1,744,404
and the net income of the Bank was $1,037,620.  The current value
of the stock is $15.00 per share as of October 10, 1990.

Mr. Quiroz also works parttime as a real estate salesman for a real
estate brokerage business which is located within the expanded
Project Area.  The real estate brokerage is a source of income to
Mr. Quiroz of $250.00 or more per year.  The real estate brokerage
firm is nearly exclusively residential sales.  Less than 2% of its
business concerns commercial transactions.  The real estate
business leases the building in which it is located.

Based on the above facts, would you please advise whether
Commissioners Alvarado, Zapien or Quiroz have a conflict of
interest which would disqualify them from participating in Pico
Rivera Redevelopment Agency matters pursuant to the

Pair Political Practices Commission
October 9, 1990
Page 3

disqualification provisions of Government Code Section 87100.

Your prompt written reply to our request for advice will be
sincerely appreciated. If you need any further information, please
do not hesitate to contact us immediately. Thank you.

Sincerely,

SAMUEL SIEGEL, CITY ATTORNEY

SN:bh

[illegible] [illegible]
